_about: Sample Features for trying out ThreatGuard's Release → Feature → Threat Model hierarchy. Create the release first (POST /api/features needs a real release_id), then create each feature under it. Field names and the `status` values match the app's Feature API (see threat-modeler/app.py → FeatureCreateRequest).
_usage: 1. Sign in and create a release:  POST /api/releases  {"name": ...}
2. Take the returned release id and create each feature:  POST /api/features  {"release_id": <id>, ...}
3. Add a threat model under any feature and analyze it (rules-only works offline).
Statuses: draft | in_review | in_sprint | released | cancelled. target_date is optional (YYYY-MM-DD).

release.name: 2026 Platform Hardening
release.description: Cross-cutting release used to threat-model the core surfaces of a typical SaaS platform.

features.name: User Authentication & SSO
features.description: Email/password login with account lockout plus OAuth/OIDC single sign-on (Google, Okta). Issues short-lived JWTs with refresh-token rotation. Threat focus: credential stuffing, token theft, session fixation.

features.name: Payment Checkout Flow
features.description: Cart checkout that tokenizes cards via a third-party processor (Stripe) and never stores PAN data. Threat focus: PCI scope, tampering with amounts, replay of payment intents, webhook spoofing.

features.name: Admin Console & RBAC
features.description: Back-office console with role-based access (user / management / admin), impersonation, and an audit log. Threat focus: privilege escalation, IDOR, missing authorization checks, audit-log tampering.

features.name: Public REST API & Webhooks
features.description: Versioned public API with API-key and OAuth auth, per-key rate limiting, and signed outbound webhooks. Threat focus: broken object-level authorization, rate-limit bypass, SSRF via webhook URLs, key leakage.

features.name: File Upload & Media Storage
features.description: Direct-to-object-store (S3) uploads with pre-signed URLs, virus scanning, and CDN delivery. Threat focus: unrestricted file upload, path traversal, content-type confusion, malware distribution.

features.name: Notification Service
features.description: Fan-out email (SendGrid), SMS gateway, and mobile push. Templates rendered from user-influenced data. Threat focus: template/HTML injection, notification spoofing, PII leakage to third parties, enumeration via delivery status.

features.name: Data Export & Reporting
features.description: Async CSV/PDF exports and a reporting warehouse (Snowflake) fed by a data pipeline. Threat focus: CSV formula injection, over-broad data exposure, insecure temporary storage, cross-tenant leakage.

features.name: Third-party OAuth Integrations
features.description: Connect external SaaS accounts (GitHub, Slack, Jira) via OAuth and store scoped tokens encrypted at rest. Threat focus: token scope creep, secret storage, CSRF on the connect flow, confused-deputy attacks.

features.name: Mobile App Backend
features.description: BFF (backend-for-frontend) API for iOS/Android with device binding, certificate pinning support, and push registration. Threat focus: insecure deep links, replay of device tokens, weak transport validation, jailbreak/root abuse.

features.name: Search & Indexing Service
features.description: Full-text search over tenant data using an external search cluster, with per-tenant index isolation. Threat focus: query injection, cross-tenant index access, denial of service via expensive queries, stale ACLs after permission changes.
